title: Laboratory Definition Standard
purpose: Single machine-readable authority for the old microcosm-as-experiment lane after the 2026-05-21 naming split. A laboratory is a controlled, fixture-backed experiment substrate that tests candidate grammar, packets, routes, receipts, validators, product mechanics, or public-safe projections before any result is promoted into live substrate.

definition.register: controlled experiment substrate with fixtures, evaluators, receipts, and explicit promotion/deletion/deferral disposition
definition.answers_question: Does this candidate shape work when enacted against representative fixtures and checked by a receipt-bearing evaluator?
is_not: Microcosm product primitive — the public-safe `repo -> .microcosm` substrate is governed by std_microcosm.json and microcosm_substrate.md
fixture — a laboratory uses fixtures but is not itself test data
paper_module — paper modules describe current substrate; laboratories test candidate future substrate
live private repo — laboratory output never bypasses the owning apply/review lane
public release gate — laboratory evidence may feed a public candidate, but release authority stays external
ad hoc prototype — laboratory waves carry a plan, receipt, and disposition

core_vocabulary.laboratory: controlled, fixture-backed experiment substrate for testing candidate system or product grammar before promotion
core_vocabulary.laboratory_probe_plan: per-wave packet naming mode, theory_ref, packet_ref, fixture_slice, candidate_question, example_case, counterexample_case, thresholds, assimilation_rule, and next_wave_selector
core_vocabulary.laboratory_probe_receipt: per-wave receipt carrying pass/partial_pass/fail/not_run evidence, opened/skipped rows, wrong_open_count, missing_field_count, authority_preserved_rows, lost_authority_cases, side findings, and assimilation decision
core_vocabulary.laboratory_learning_ledger: per-wave learning surface preserving anchors, packet seam tested, receipt id, observed gap, and next-wave selector
core_vocabulary.assimilation_lift: controller-owned promotion from a passing laboratory result into the owning doctrine, standard, skill, paper module, code, or public Microcosm lane

governance.writer: agent or human proposal; field-set additions or enum changes after v0 require v1 review

applies_to: laboratory probe receipts and learning ledgers
legacy system_microcosm_probe and microcosm_autonomous_seed_plan compatibility surfaces
does_not_apply_to: microcosm-substrate/ as the current public product primitive
self-indexing-cognitive-substrate release-root historical framing except where an owner route selects it as archaeology
raw fixture data without a governed experiment plan and receipt

hard_invariants.evidence_not_authority: Laboratory output is evidence, never authority; promotion is a separate lift through the target kind's existing owner lane.
hard_invariants.microcosm_name_reserved: Bare Microcosm names the current public-safe product/substrate compression, not the old experiment lane.
hard_invariants.receipt_required: A substantive laboratory wave closes with pass, partial_pass, fail, not_run with blocker, deletion, deferral, or promotion-candidate evidence.
hard_invariants.source_hidden_before_promotion: A candidate grammar should pass source-hidden example and counterexample checks before promotion.
hard_invariants.public_claims_external: Laboratory proof cannot authorize public release, hosted claims, source-available language, or private-root equivalence.

relation_to_neighbors.microcosm: Microcosm is the public-safe product/substrate compression of the macro system, currently centered on microcosm-substrate/ and `repo -> .microcosm`. Laboratory is the private/control experiment lane that may improve Microcosm but does not define it.
relation_to_neighbors.imagination: An imagination renders a counterfactual affordance scene; a laboratory tests an enacted candidate against fixtures.
relation_to_neighbors.paper_module: A paper module describes a current subsystem; a laboratory tests whether a candidate future subsystem shape should be promoted.
relation_to_neighbors.fixture: A fixture is test data; a laboratory is the governed experiment using fixtures.
relation_to_neighbors.public_constellation_strategy: Public gates can consume laboratory evidence but remain separate release/disclosure authorities.

migration_note.to: laboratory for controlled experiment substrate; Microcosm for public product/substrate primitive
migration_note.compatibility_policy: Keep legacy ids temporarily where renaming would break generated routes or mission templates; visible prose and vocabulary should prefer Laboratory.
